texto: We've been down the road of your hasty exits too many times, Mrs. Danvers. You married your master, and you married a sadist--of your own free will. You might remember that when you're tempted to walk out in a huff, defy my orders, and behave like a selfish brat. You got that?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lizbeth Dusseau: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “We've been down the road of your hasty exits too many times, Mrs. Danvers. You married your maste…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, bdsm, dubious-consent pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e dubious-consent.
